metrics.narrative: Tony Singleton has repeatedly said he lacks the baseline data (requisition volumes, PO-to-conversion times, cost) needed to build a credible ROI business case: "I don't have any data which tells them how many requisitions are being raised, how long it takes for them to convert to a PO... I've gotta start trying to pull that data together first" (23 Feb 2026 call). No dollar figure or quantified impact has been stated by AAK in any call read this sweep. The qualitative pain is well documented (manual, multi-ERP process, ~80% spend-data accuracy), but nothing has been quantified. The move is to help Tony finish pulling that baseline data so a real business case can be built before the RFI/RFP concludes.

champion.narrative: Tony Singleton is the only confirmed, actively engaged stakeholder: he has attended every call since 23 Feb 2026, brought colleagues into two live demos (10 Mar and 25 Mar 2026), and asked detailed integration questions about ERP push, punch-out routing and PO receipt sync (25 Mar 2026), signaling real evaluation effort. What has NOT yet developed: any evidence he has advocated for Zycus upward to the Swedish procurement leadership he says he sits alongside, or that he has pushed back on a competitor in Zycus's favor. His champion strength is developing, not yet proven, pending that upward advocacy.

competition.narrative: Salesforce lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba as competitors, consistent with Tony's own statement that he is running a structured comparison across "4-5 solution providers" (25 Mar 2026) and "can talk to you and another half a dozen different providers" (23 Feb 2026). No call read this sweep names a specific rival directly or states a buyer preference between them, so ranking is carried forward from prior analysis: JAGGAER is flagged as the sharpest current risk because of its strength in services procurement, directly relevant to AAK's unresolved rate-card-for-services question (25 Mar 2026). Zycus's wedge is Merlin/agentic AI and the guided-buying Teams experience, which the AAK team responded to positively on both demos.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well and specifically articulated by Tony: "Procurement... is very immature... very manual" across US, Mexico, Colombia and Brazil on different ERPs (23 Feb 2026); purchase orders routinely lack part numbers or descriptions, "making accurate spend analysis impossible" (2 Mar 2026); no enforced preferred-supplier catalog exists, leading to what Tony calls "rogue spend"; and the current VAI ERP has an all-or-nothing approval threshold that can't be tuned by purchase type (2 Mar 2026). Tony owns this pain directly as AAK's US Procurement Manager.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec) have been discussed in any call read this sweep; the joint evaluation plan Tony agreed to on 2 Mar 2026 shows contract draft and execution only as the final downstream steps, well after demos and pricing. Given the deal is at Shortlisted, this is expected at this point in the cycle rather than a red flag, but it means zero visibility exists yet on legal/procurement mechanics for AAK, or on data-residency requirements for the cross-border (US/Sweden) data the platform would touch.

economic_buyer.narrative: No named economic buyer has ever appeared on a Zycus call. Tony describes a Swedish-based "procurement excellence team" and director who own the global 2027-2030 eProcurement roadmap and who Tony must align with before committing: "I have a global element that I need to also kind of align with and see how that works" (2 Mar 2026). Tony himself sits on the global procurement leadership team, which is a positive proxy for budget influence, but there is no evidence he has secured, or even scheduled, sign-off from that Swedish leadership. This is the single largest unresolved MEDDPICC gap on the deal; the move is a direct introduction to that Swedish role.

decision_process.narrative: Justin Ajmo laid out a joint evaluation plan on 2 Mar 2026 (intro call -> discovery -> demo -> pricing -> additional demos if needed -> implementation scoping -> contract draft -> execute), which Tony agreed matched his thinking for the near term. AAK is running a formal, multi-vendor process: "Currently in discovery phase with 4-5 solution providers under evaluation" (25 Mar 2026 key takeaways), and Zycus was later told it had been shortlisted (Next Step, 12 May 2026). The gap is that AAK's own RFI/RFP timeline and down-select date have never been shared with Zycus despite being explicitly requested as a next step on 25 Mar 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: Tony named his top 3 evaluation priorities on 2 Mar 2026: (1) enforcing preferred-supplier usage and stopping rogue spend, (2) improving policy compliance, and (3) improving spend-data categorization beyond the current ~80% accuracy. Ritika confirmed Zycus's fit on the first two directly on the call. A newer, un-resolved criterion surfaced on 25 Mar 2026: whether service/professional-services rate cards can be managed like catalog items, which is not yet confirmed as supported.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus lines up well against AAK's top 3 stated priorities (preferred-supplier enforcement, policy compliance, spend-data accuracy), but a real gap remains on rate-card-based services purchasing, and ROI/business-case validation is still pending AAK's own baseline data.

hard.ais_why:
• On the 3/25/2026 call, Tony Singleton was unambiguous about deprioritizing AI intake: the majority of users will bypass Merlin Intake and go straight to eProcurement, with only roughly 2% of occasional business users as Merlin candidates. He framed AI intake as useful only for the occasional non-procurement user, not for the 98% who buy from catalog.
• Tony also voiced a direct quality objection on the demo, saying the perceived AI slowness raised concerns about responsiveness, and noted that a competitor had demoed faster direct quote upload inside eProcurement. The buyer is actively benchmarking against AI alternatives and finds Zycus's flow slower today.
• The RFP itself is rated "No AI Requirement." The scope is catalog management, punch-outs to Grainger and CDW, ERP receipt flow, and rate cards: classic rules-based eProcurement. No AI POC, no AI-tied budget, no AI champion.
hard.next_step: JRA 5/12: Invited Tony to Agentic AI Procurement Summit. Offering my hand in helping him create the requirements document for the vendors in the eval, as Zycus has already been notified of being shortlisted.
